LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS PATRICK DUCKWALD AND DENIZ DUCKWALD'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-08-626919 WITH CASE CGC-08-472638 IS GRANTED CONDITIONED ON THE FOLLOWING: 1- THE HEARING DATE FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTIONS WILL BE DEC-16-2008. MOVING PAPERS TO BE FILED BY NOV-14-2008, OPPOSITION BY DEC-5-2008, AND REPLY BY DEC-12-2008; 2- PARTIES ARE TO REQUEST THAT THE PRESIDING JUDGE SET A TRIAL DATE TO BEGIN SOON AFTER DEC-16-2008; AND 3- WRITTEN DISCOVERY WILL HAVE A 15 DAY RESPONSE PERIOD. MOTIONS CAN BE SET ON 10 DAYS NOTICE, WITH OPPOSITION DUE 5 DAYS PRIOR TO THE HEARING AND REPLY DUE THE DAY BEFORE THE HEARING. CONSOLIDATION IS FOR ALL PURPOSES. LEAD CASE WILL BE CGC-08-472638. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-COMPLAINANT HANUS JELINEK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THE EXISTENCE OF A LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP FOR THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. THE PARTIES' COMPLICATED ARRANGEMENT CAN BE CHARACTERIZED AS A CONTINUATION OF THE TENANCY IN COMMON RELATIONSHIP. JELINEK HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE ON THE EJECTMENT ACTION AND DUCKWALD HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON JELINEK'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO PERFORM THE TRUST AGREEMENT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-COMPLAINANT HANUS JELINEK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Keith M. Velleca, Esq. of Law Offices of Keith M. Velleca 974-1200 for opposing party Patrick Duckwald; Kenneth Johnson, Esq. of Jones, Clifford, Johnson & Johnson LLP 431-5310 for opposing party Deniz Duckwald; Scott A. Freedman, Esq. of Zacks Utrecht & Leadbetter 956-8100 for moving party Hanus Jelinek.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THE EXISTENCE OF A LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP FOR THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. THE PARTIES' COMPLICATED ARRANGEMENT CAN BE CHARACTERIZED AS A CONTINUATION OF THE TENANCY IN COMMON RELATIONSHIP. JELINEK HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE ON THE EJECTMENT ACTION AND DUCKWALD HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON JELINEK'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO PERFORM THE TRUST AGREEMENT. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
